items.headline: NVIDIA stock retakes $5T market cap (Apr 24 close)
items.diff_narrative: **New: NVIDIA stock retakes $5T market cap (Apr 24 close)** — Last report had Intel Q1 2026's blowout + AH +20%. Now NVIDIA closes the regular session at **$208.27 (+4.3%) for $5.12T market cap**, with AMD +13.90% and S&P 500 / Nasdaq Composite both at records the same day. Day 2 of the Intel beat → sector chain becomes the financial-market headline.

items.headline: AMD +13.90% ahead of earnings (May 5) (Apr 24)
items.diff_narrative: **New: AMD +13.90% ahead of earnings (May 5) (Apr 24)** — Not in the previous report. AMD closes +13.9% at $347.77 in a single day. Q1 2026 print is **May 5**; estimates are revenue $9.84B / EPS YoY +33%. May earnings rally lifts NVIDIA / Intel / Broadcom / Micron in chain.

items.headline: Tesla Earth Day (Apr 25) + Optimus Plant Cube giveaway
items.diff_narrative: **New: Tesla Earth Day (Apr 25) + Optimus Plant Cube giveaway** — Last report had Tesla Q1 2026 results and Optimus V3 mass-production confirmation. Now we have **Apr 25 Earth Day** at U.S. flagship stores giving FSD (Supervised) demo-drive participants an **Optimus-planted Plant Cube** — an early-stage maneuver to put Optimus into customers' hands physically.

items.headline: Google Cloud × Thinking Machines Lab GB300 deal in the multi-billions (announced Apr 22)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Google Cloud × Thinking Machines Lab GB300 deal in the multi-billions (announced Apr 22)** — Last report had general announcements through Google Cloud Next '26 Day 3. Now we have **Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab adopting NVIDIA GB300-powered Google Cloud A4X Max, training / serving speed 2x.** Third frontier-lab deal after Anthropic and Meta, central to Google Cloud's AI Hypercomputer strategy.

items.headline: BMW Plant Leipzig AEON humanoid trials begin in April 2026 + Physical AI Center of Competence opens
items.diff_narrative: **New: BMW Plant Leipzig AEON humanoid trials begin in April 2026 + Physical AI Center of Competence opens** — Last report had Siemens × Humanoid Erlangen factory's 8h production run. Now Spartanburg Figure 02's record of **10 months / 30,000 vehicles / 90,000 parts / 1,250 hours** is locked, and Europe's first Physical AI hub opens in Leipzig. Hexagon Robotics' **AEON** enters trial from April 2026.

items.headline: Hannover Messe 2026 (Apr 20-24) closes with Physical AI as first central theme
items.diff_narrative: **New: Hannover Messe 2026 (Apr 20-24) closes with Physical AI as first central theme** — Last report covered through Day 3. Now closing at **130,000 visitors / 4,000 exhibitors / 1,600 speakers**, with the concept of "AI leaving the screen and entering the physical world" dominating the floor. AEON / HMND 01 / Apptronik Apollo / Agility Digit are exhibited side by side.

items.headline: Claude Code v2.1.117 (released Apr 25)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Claude Code v2.1.117 (released Apr 25) — `/resume` auto-summary + model persistence strengthening** — Last report ended at v2.1.116 quality recovery. Now **v2.1.117** auto-summarizes stalled / large sessions before reload to prevent context overflow, aligned with `--resume` behavior. A model selection different from the project pin persists across restarts; the boot header shows the model's source. Forking subagent enabled by external builds (`CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1`); mcpServers loaded via `--agent`.

items.headline: OpenClaw v2026.4.23 (Apr 24 15:19 tag)
items.diff_narrative: **New: OpenClaw v2026.4.23 (Apr 24 15:19 tag) — image gen via Codex OAuth + OpenRouter image gen** — Last report had v2026.4.22. Now **v2026.4.23** adds **image generation + reference-image editing via Codex OAuth** to Providers/OpenAI (no OPENAI_API_KEY needed for `openai/gpt-image-2`), and **image generation + reference-image editing via the `image_generate` API** to Providers/OpenRouter (OPENROUTER_API_KEY runs OpenRouter image models).

items.headline: CISA adds 4 to KEV on Apr 24 (Samsung MagicINFO / SimpleHelp x2 / D-Link)
items.diff_narrative: **New: CISA adds 4 to KEV on Apr 24 (Samsung MagicINFO / SimpleHelp x2 / D-Link)** — Last report had Apr 22's Marimo Pre-Auth RCE addition. Now **CVE-2024-7399 / CVE-2024-57726 / CVE-2024-57728 / CVE-2025-29635** are added as actively exploited; old-but-active MSP / RMM / industrial-display / home-router vulns are back in focus.

items.headline: Nginx UI CVE-2026-33032 (MCPwn, CVSS 9.8) actively exploited
items.diff_narrative: **New: Nginx UI CVE-2026-33032 (MCPwn, CVSS 9.8) actively exploited** — Last report had SGLang CVE-2026-5760 as the highest-severity MCP / GGUF-related CVE. Now we have **a structural defect in MCP protocol integration itself**: the `/mcp_message` endpoint with an empty default IP allowlist allows 12 unauthenticated MCP tool calls → 2 HTTP requests to fully take over Nginx. Recorded Future lists it among Top 31 actively exploited CVEs in March 2026; Shodan shows **2,600+ instances exposed**.

items.headline: Saltcorn CVE-2026-41478 (CVSS 9.9) SQL Injection disclosed (Apr 24)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Saltcorn CVE-2026-41478 (CVSS 9.9) SQL Injection disclosed (Apr 24)** — Not in the previous report. **Saltcorn's Mobile-Sync feature lets an authenticated low-privilege user inject arbitrary SQL, leaking the full DB including admin password hashes / config secrets**. Fixed in 1.4.6 / 1.5.6 / 1.6.0-beta.5.

items.headline: Salesforce Q4 EPS / Agentforce ARR $800M / 29,000 deals (+50% QoQ)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Salesforce Q4 EPS / Agentforce ARR $800M / 29,000 deals (+50% QoQ)** — Last report had ServiceNow's -18% worst-ever single-day drop. Now **Salesforce reports FY2026 revenue $41.5B, Agentforce ARR $800M, 29,000 deals (+50% QoQ)**, Q4 EPS $3.81 / revenue $11.20B (+12.1% YoY) — sharpening the "decline vs accelerate" split with ServiceNow.

items.headline: DeepSeek V4 Pro detailed benchmarks (Apr 24)
items.diff_narrative: **New: DeepSeek V4 Pro detailed benchmarks (Apr 24)** — Last report had V4 Pro / Flash overall specs and pricing. Now **IMOAnswerBench 89.8 (above Claude 75.3 / Gemini 81.0, close to GPT-5.4 91.4)**, agentic above Claude Sonnet 4.5 and Opus 4.5-class self-rated, "marginally short" on reasoning vs GPT-5.4 / Gemini 3.1-Pro.

items.headline: AI Tinkerers SF + AI Dev 26 x SF + Sage Future running back-to-back (Apr 28-30)
items.diff_narrative: **New: AI Tinkerers SF + AI Dev 26 x SF + Sage Future running back-to-back (Apr 28-30)** — Not in the previous report. Now **Apr 29 AI Tinkerers SF VIP Dinner (Building Software Factories with Kiro & B-Capital)**, **Apr 28-29 AI Dev 26 x SF @ Pier 48 (3,000+ developers)**, and **Apr 28-30 Sage Future @ Moscone Center** all run in the same week — the Bay Area AI engineer / builder scene converges around the wave of GPT-5.5 / DeepSeek V4 releases.

items.headline: Amazon × Anthropic added $25B + 5GW Trainium2/3 capacity (Apr 20)
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: Amazon × Anthropic added $25B + 5GW Trainium2/3 capacity (Apr 20)** — Last report had only the background mention. Now we have detail confirmed: **5GW Trainium capacity, 1GW Trainium2/3 simultaneous run by year-end 2026, $5B immediate + $20B milestone-tied, Anthropic 10-year $100B commit to AWS** — alongside Amazon's $50B OpenAI bet, the two biggest AI training-capacity commitments in the field.
